channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: How top Syrian diplomat Asaad al-Shaibani’s road to Damascus is shaking the Mideast
item.description: Syrian Foreign Minister Asaad al-Shaibani’s diplomatic motorcade hit the road shortly after his appointment. From Riyadh to Beirut, Washington to Moscow, Shaibani has been at the forefront of President Ahmed al-Sharaa’s bid to open a new chapter in Mideast history. But the rebel official-turned-diplomat may find Damascus does not hold all the cards in a volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Leela JACINTO

item.title: Medieval masterpiece Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London
item.description: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in London for the first time in more than 900 years after a highly secure and complex journey from France to the British Museum, as FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi explains. The 11th-century masterpiece depicting the Battle of Hastings will be displayed from September 2026 to July 2027, in a historic loan hailed as a symbol of Franco-British ties.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Wildfires rage across Spain as heatwave fuels flames
item.description: At least 11 people have died as wildfires spread across Spain, with firefighters battling flames from the south to the north of the country and evacuations underway in several areas. The blazes come amid a severe heatwave, with temperatures above 40°C recorded across Spain and strong winds making efforts to contain the fires more difficult.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: The Bayeux Tapestry’s extraordinary journey back to Britain
item.description: After nearly 1,000 years, the Bayeux Tapestry has returned to the UK for a year-long exhibition at the British Museum. Its highly secretive and carefully controlled journey involved police escorts, a specially designed case and strict conditions to protect the fragile medieval masterpiece, in a move seen as a symbol of Franco-British ties.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Can Ukraine really build its own Patriots?
item.description: Donald Trump’s promise to let Ukraine manufacture Patriot air defence systems could strengthen Kyiv’s capabilities in the long term, but experts warn it will not solve the country’s immediate battlefield needs. Building production lines, training workers and securing supplies could take years, while Ukraine continues to face daily Russian attacks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Foreign workers feel the heat as Japan hardens nationalist stance
item.description: On this week's edition of Access Asia, we bring you a report from Japan, where visa fees have been increased for foreign nationals applying for permanent residency. The government is also considering introducing a language requirement. While Japan needs foreign workers to help offset an ageing population and a declining birth rate, the nationalist government has continued to tighten its immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: Joanna COCKERELL

item.title: World Cup 2026 : Les Bleus masterclass sends France to semi finals
item.description: Morocco struggled to show much danger in the first half and spent most of the time in a low block to try to counter the French onslaught. Mbappé won a penalty in the 28th minute after being fouled by Manchester United defender Noussair Mazraoui. After three minutes of a confusingly prolonged VAR check, his spot-kick was comfortably saved by Yassine Bounou. The first half finished scoreless, but the tone of the match was set, Morocco didn't log a single shot on target. Mbappé made amends in the 60th minute with a brilliant goal, curled past a defender to find the back of the Moroccan net, making it his eighth goal at the tournament. Ousmane Dembéle doubled down six minutes later, and the score line stayed at 2-0 until the final whistle. France are through to the semi finals for a third time in a row.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: France faces third major heatwave since May
item.description: As another intense heatwave sweeps across France, residents are staying indoors, flocking to canals and rivers, or even leaving their homes in search of cooler places. With temperatures reaching 41.1°C in Lodève and almost the entire country under heat alerts, holidaymakers and locals alike fear the worst is still to come.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran ramps up regional pressure after renewed US strikes
item.description: Following fresh US strikes during the funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran stepped up pressure by targeting US allies and threatening American bases across the region in an effort to force Washington back to the negotiating table. According to FRANCE 24's Philip Turle, despite Donald Trump's claim that the ceasefire is over, backchannel talks are likely continuing, as neither side can afford a prolonged conflict, particularly with US midterm elections approaching.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France and Morocco set for blockbuster World Cup showdown
item.description: France face Morocco for a place in the World Cup semi-finals, with Les Bleus starting as favourites but expecting a tough test from Achraf Hakimi and his teammates. France 24 speaks to The Peninsula Qatar's Victor Bolorunduro about the key battles, the refereeing controversies surrounding the tournament and why it has still been one of the most entertaining World Cups in recent years.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran: Anti-US anger dominates Khamenei funeral ceremonies
item.description: Millions of mourners have taken part in six days of funeral ceremonies for Iran's former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, chanting anti-US and anti-Israel slogans as the country commemorates the leader killed in US-Israeli strikes in late February. Even as the ceremonies drew to a close, fresh US strikes fuelled tensions, while Iran's new supreme leader remained out of public sight throughout the mourning period.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France reach a third straight World Cup semi-final
item.description: France booked their place in a third consecutive FIFA World Cup semi-final with a 2-0 win over Morocco, filling the nation with pride as Les Bleus moved one step closer to glory and set up a last-four clash with either Belgium or Spain. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes has more from Boston.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France book their place in the World Cup semi-finals
item.description: France marched into the FIFA World Cup semi-finals with a convincing 2-0 win over Morocco, leaving fans across the country bursting with pride as Les Bleus moved within two matches of lifting football's biggest trophy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Brazil: Sao Paulo's AI surveillance system accused of racial profiling
item.description: Under the guise of fighting crime, Sao Paulo has rolled out the world's largest facial recognition system operated by a city. India, China, Hong Kong and the United Kingdom have introduced similar technologies under vague legal frameworks. But no city has embraced surveillance on the same scale as Sao Paulo, making it the world's biggest "Big Brother". The artificial intelligence system, known as Smart Sampa, is now at the centre of a growing scandal after being accused of racial profiling. Report by Marine Resse, Fanny Lothaire, Mélanie Blondeau.
item.dc:creator: Marine RESSE

item.title: World Cup 2026: Paris celebrates France's win against Morocco
item.description: For the third time in a row, #France qualified for the #WorldCup semifinal after beating #Morocco 2-0 on July 9. #Paris erupted with joy as the sound of the final whistle, with many fans already seeing themselves celebrating the final.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Facebook and Instagram must stop ‘addictive’ features causing infinite scrolling, EU tells Meta
item.description: Meta, the parent company of the popular social media platforms Facebook and Instagram, must change the design features that make them addictive to users, the European Union said Friday. It also threatened to slap the US tech giant with steep fines if it does not abide by the new directive.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: A cultural stroll through Paris with Kalash and Manon Lisa
item.description: A summer of music with Paris des Arts! On today's show, we meet up with French rapper Kalash as well as singer Manon Lisa.
item.dc:creator: Valérie FAYOLLE

item.title: The dark side of the World Cup: A rise in domestic violence
item.description: While millions follow the World Cup, studies reveal a noticeable uptick in domestic abuse causing women’s rights activists to raise the flag. Annette Young also talks to FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes, who's covering the World Cup, about the issues women journalists face in covering such events. Also in Pakistan, a small but growing DJ scene is transforming the music industry in this traditionally conservative country with a growing number of women leading the charge.
item.dc:creator: Sonia BARITELLO

item.title: Defence spending, Erdogan-style: Guns for NATO leaders
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, July 10: the French press react to another World Cup victory for the Blues, this time against Morocco. In other news, the Boeing jet gifted to Donald Trump by the Qatari state has occasioned new controversy, as it may not have the necessary safety features to transport a President. And, finally, NATO leaders express gratitude and confusion at President Erdogan's parting gift: an engraved pistol with 6 live bullets.
item.dc:creator: Antonia KERRIGAN

item.title: What future for Gaza after Hamas dissolves governing body?
item.description: Hamas announced this week that it had dissolved the de facto government, saying it was ready to hand over power to a group of technocrats appointed by Donald Trump's Board of Peace. Israel dismissed the move as a stunt. But with the Israeli military continuing to bomb Gaza despite the ostensible ceasefire, while controlling more than 60 per cent of the territory, and with the technocratic committee still outside the enclave, who is really in charge?
item.dc:creator: Nadia MASSIH

item.title: Iran had 'specific' plan to kill Trump, US media report citing Israeli intel
item.description: Iran planned to kill US President Donald Trump in a fresh plot unveiled by Israel, several US media outlets report. According to Israeli intelligence, the plan was “specific” and may explain why Trump chose to switch planes when leaving the NATO summit in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Spain confront Belgium in World Cup quarter-finals, winner to face France
item.description: Spain and Belgium are set to face off in the World Cup quarter-finals on Friday. The winner of the match will go on to be tested against Kylian Mbappé's formidable France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France book World Cup semi-final place with win over Morocco thanks to Mbappé, Dembélé
item.description: Les Bleus advanced to the World Cup semi-finals for the third consecutive time on Thursday. Didier Deschamps' side struggled for the first 45 minutes against Morocco’s defence before making the breakthrough in the second half thanks to Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé (2-0). They will face Spain or Belgium for a spot in the final.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: 🔴Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
